title: Hippeastrum correiense
intro: Hippeastrum correiense, also known as the Easter lily, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family. The plant is rich in bioactive constituents, including alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which have been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant's bioactive constituents, such as galantamine and lycorine, have been used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The most common herbal preparations made with Hippeastrum correiense include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
